Description: RQ100	Requirements shall be managed in a 3rd party requirements management tool such as DOORS.
Reasoning: Demonstrates integration with DOORS to retrieve requirement data but does not assert comprehensive management policies such as that all requirements are stored there, CRUD enforcement, or DOORS outage handling.

Description: RQ101	Non-requirement artifacts shall be managed in 3rd party tools such as Rational Rose or Clear case
Reasoning: None of the provided tests mention or assert integration with third-party tools (Rational Rose, ClearCase) or the management of non-requirement artifacts within such tools. The candidate tests focus on trace establishment, subscriber behavior, event processing, and requirement lifecycle operations (delete, decompose, refine), so there is no assertion evidence covering RQ101.

Description: RQ102	Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reasoning: Direct positive postcondition showing a registered subscriber can successfully establish a trace, which maps to the requirement's allowed behavior.

Description: RQ102	Only registered subscribers shall be allowed to establish traces.
Reasoning: Direct negative postcondition showing an unregistered subscriber is prevented from establishing a trace, which maps to the requirement's denied behavior.

Description: RQ103	A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reasoning: Direct match: the test step explicitly states the subscriber performs registration and the postcondition asserts it is registered under the subscriber manager.

Description: RQ103	A user shall register as a subscriber.
Reasoning: Partial coverage: this exercises the abort/failure path of registration but does not assert successful registration or resulting persistent state.

Description: RQ104	On registration a subscriber shall register itself under the control of a subscriber manager
Reasoning: Directly asserts registration and a postcondition that the subscriber is registered under the subscriber manager, but does not assert or exercise that the subscriber is 'under the control' (manager-issued control operations or lifecycle changes).

Description: RQ105	The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reasoning: Directly asserts a trace is established (core behavior) but does not specify the required subscription mechanism or the artifact->requirement directionality.

Description: RQ105	The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reasoning: Provides a negative/error case showing trace creation can fail (authorization/registration) but does not demonstrate an artifact-initiated subscription or its exact semantics.

Description: RQ105	The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reasoning: Demonstrates subscription semantics (subscriber manager -> requirement) and notification but does not assert the artifact-as-subscriber (artifact -> requirement) subscription required by RQ105.

Description: RQ105	The user shall establish traces between requirements and other types of artifacts by establishing a subscription from an artifact to the relevant requirement
Reasoning: Implied use of artifact-requirement links and subscriber notification is shown, but there is no assertion of the user action creating an artifact->requirement subscription.

Description: RQ106	Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reasoning: Directly asserts registration of a 'subscriber' under a subscriber manager but does not assert placement for the generic class 'each artifact' or other artifact types.

Description: RQ106	Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reasoning: Provides negative-case enforcement for subscribers but does not prove universal placement or handling for other artifact classes.

Description: RQ106	Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reasoning: Indicates a subscriber manager has subscriptions to requirements (a type of artifact) but is weak evidence for the universal requirement that each artifact is placed under a subscriber manager.

Description: RQ106	Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reasoning: Assumes registered subscribers exist under a manager (precondition) but lacks an explicit assertion that all artifacts are placed under a subscriber manager.

Description: RQ106	Each artifact shall be placed under the control of a subscriber manager
Reasoning: Shows linking of requirements to artifacts and routing to subscribers but does not assert that every artifact is placed under manager control or that placement is enforced for all artifact types.

Description: RQ107	The user shall be able to delete subscriptions.
Reasoning: The test directly asserts the postcondition that 'The subscription has been deleted', satisfying the core happy path but not additional error or edge cases.

Description: RQ108	A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reasoning: Demonstrates the manager can retrieve notifications after coming online, but does not document an explicit registration action with the eventserver.

Description: RQ108	A subscriber manager shall register itself with the eventserver so that it may receive relevant event messages.
Reasoning: Shows the eventserver knows subscriber managers and sends notifications, but does not assert the subscriber manager-initiated registration call or acknowledgement.

Description: RQ109	If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reasoning: Explicitly asserts the online condition, labels the event as published in real time, and states the server sends the notification, directly matching the requirement's condition, push action, and timing aspect.

Description: RQ109	If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reasoning: Shows receipt and storage of server-sent notifications (consistent with push) but "does not assert online state or real-time timing explicitly."

Description: RQ109	If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reasoning: Documents receipt and processing by the subscriber manager indicating push-like behavior, but "lacks explicit online precondition and timing guarantee."

Description: RQ109	If the subscriber manager is online; the push model shall be used to send it event notifications in real time.
Reasoning: Documents server-initiated sends and receipt (push model), but "lacks an explicit online precondition and explicit 'real time' measurement/guarantee".

Description: RQ110	Whenever the subscriber manager first comes on line; it shall implement the pull model to retrieve outstanding event messages from the eventserver.
Reasoning: The test directly constructs the offline-with-undelivered-messages scenario, transitions the subscriber manager online, and asserts successful download of those outstanding messages.

Description: RQ111	A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.
Reasoning: 'The relevant subscriber manager receives the event notification' and 'stored in the local event log' are both asserted in the test text.

Description: RQ111	A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.

Description: RQ111	A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.

Description: RQ111	A subscriber manager shall receive event notifications on behalf of the artifacts that it manages.

Description: RQ112	A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.

Description: RQ112	A subscriber manager shall manage traces for one or more artifacts.

Description: RQ113	On receipt of an event message; the subscriber manager shall differentiate between speculative and evolutionary events.

Description: RQ123	Event types shall include refining one requirement by adding an additional requirement to the existing one.
Reasoning: The test steps and post-condition explicitly describe refining an existing requirement by adding an additional related requirement, directly satisfying the requirement.

Description: RQ124	Event types shall include modifying a requirement.

Description: RQ125	Event types shall include deleting a requirement.

Description: RQ139	The user shall eliminate unnecessary traces that were temporarily constructed by the eventserver during initial event processing.
